channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Texas death row inmate mouths final 2-word message to victims' families before execution
item.description: A remorseful death row inmate pleaded for forgiveness and mothed one final message before being put to death in Texas Thursday, 20 years after he killed his strip club manager and another man.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: 3 Israeli men named as latest to be freed in hostage release
item.description: The families of Israeli hostages still held by the Hamas militant group have released the names of three hostages expected to be freed on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Where US adults think the government is spending too much, according to AP-NORC polling
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Many U.S. adults believe the federal government is overspending — but polling also shows that many Americans, including Republicans, think the country is spending too little on major government programs such as Social Security.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AP Decision Notes: What to expect in Wisconsin's spring primary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Wisconsin’s top school official faces the first big hurdle of her reelection bid in the spring primary on Tuesday, when she’s running against two challengers in a far different political and electoral environment than she did in her successful 2021 campaign.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT YOON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Prosecutors see an Islamic extremist motive in the Munich car-ramming attack
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — The suspect in the car-ramming attack in Munich that left more than 30 people injured appears to have had an Islamic extremist motive, but there's no evidence that he was involved with any radical network, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIPP JENNE and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Order to drop New York mayor's case rocks Justice Department as high-ranking officials resign
item.description: Manhattan’s top federal prosecutor, Danielle Sassoon, and five high-ranking Justice Department officials resigned Thursday after she refused an order to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Southern California hit by destructive debris flows caused by heavy rains
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Southern California faces the continued risk of rock and mudslides on wildfire-scarred hillsides Friday, a day after heavy rains sent debris across several roadways, including the Pacific Coast Highway, where a fire department vehicle was pushed into the ocean.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Man to plead guilty to 2023 shooting of Black teen Ralph Yarl, 2 people familiar with case tell AP
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — An 86-year-old Kansas City man will plead guilty Friday to the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the man’s doorbell by mistake, two people familiar with the case told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: KIA BREAUX and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Plane carrying Secretary of State Rubio to Europe turned around because of a mechanical issue
item.description: An Air Force plane carrying Secretary of State Marco Rubio and the Senate Foreign Relations Committee chairman, Sen. Jim Risch, to Germany for the Munich Security Conference was forced to return to Washington late Thursday after developing a mechanical issue.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US Park Service erases the word 'transgender' from website commemorating Stonewall riot
item.description: References to transgender people were removed Thursday from a National Park Service website for the Stonewall National Monument, a park and visitor center in New York that commemorates a 1969 riot that became a pivotal moment for the LGBTQ+ rights movement.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Private border wall in South Texas still standing despite fraud by group fundraisers
item.description: Border Report revisited a privately built 3.5-mile segment of border wall south of Mission, Texas, after Steve Bannon pleaded guilty for fraud in a crowdsourcing scheme that raised millions of dollars for the project but paid very little for the building of the wall.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Florida man is executed for the 1997 killing of a couple whose toddler witnessed the attack
item.description: STARKE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida man convicted of killing a husband and wife at a remote farm in an attack witnessed by the couple’s toddler was put to death Thursday in the state's first execution of the year. James Dennis Ford, 64, was pronounced dead at 6:19 p.m.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fourth federal judge blocks Trump’s birthright citizenship order
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge in Boston on Thursday blocked an executive order from President Donald Trump that would end birthright citizenship for the children of parents who are in the U.S. illegally, becoming the fourth judge to do so. The ruling from U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man to plead guilty to 2023 shooting of Black teen Ralph Yarl, 2 people familiar with case tell AP
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — An 86-year-old Kansas City man will plead guilty Friday to the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the man’s doorbell by mistake, two people familiar with the case told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: KIA BREAUX and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man accused of shooting Kansas City teen Ralph Yarl to change plea
item.description: Andrew Lester, the man accused of shooting Ralph Yarl, a Kansas City teen in 2023, will take a plea deal in a last-minute hearing on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal judge pauses President Trump's order restricting gender-affirming care for trans youth
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s recent executive order aimed at restricting gender-affirming health care for transgender people under age 19.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dozens of vehicles involved in Oregon highway crash amid 'whiteout' conditions
item.description: Dozens of vehicles, including cars, trucks and semi-trucks, were involved in crashes along Oregon's Interstate 84 early Thursday afternoon, according to the Multnomah County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Plante, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration begins sweeping layoffs with probationary workers, warns of larger cuts to come
item.description: The Trump administration is intensifying its sweeping efforts to shrink the size of the federal workforce by ordering agencies to lay off all probationary employees who had not yet gained civil service protection.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers, White House says
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5% to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, Fox 5

item.title: Alaska US senators introduce bill that would again designate North America's tallest peak as Denali
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Alaska's Republican U.S. senators have introduced legislation seeking to designate North America’s tallest peak as Denali — weeks after President Donald Trump issued an executive order calling for the name to revert to Mount McKinley. U.S. Sen.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AI-powered fitness revolutionizing workouts with adaptive equipment
item.description: “I’m a tech nerd really at heart, and it’s cool to be on that cutting edge,” said Trevor Gere, co-owner of The Smart Fit Method in Northwest Suburban Kildeer.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Barnes, NewsNation

item.title: NYC subway rider who gave birth on train identified as missing Florida woman: report
item.description: A woman who gave birth on a New York City subway has been identified as missing woman out of Florida who was last seen by her family in August 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bristling at 'Gulf of Mexico' name change on maps, Mexico says it might sue Google
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum says her government is not ruling out filing a civil lawsuit against Google if it maintains its stance of calling the stretch of sea between northeastern Mexico and the southeastern United States the “Gulf of America.”.
item.dc:creator: Fabiola Sânchez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Indiana student accused of planning Valentine's Day school shooting
item.description: MOORESVILLE, Ind. – An 18-year-old Mooresville High School student who had a collage of mass shooters in the student’s bedroom was arrested on Wednesday for allegedly planning a school shooting on Valentine’s Day.
item.dc:creator: David Gay, NewsNation

item.title: Igloo recalling 1M+ coolers after fingertip amputations
item.description: Igloo has received 12 reports of fingertip injuries, including fingertip amputations, bone fractures and slashes.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: What's an atmospheric river? A pineapple express? AP explains the weather phenomenon
item.description: Atmospheric rivers are a plume of moisture that extend across the Pacific to near Hawaii. When they hit land they can cause heavy rains and snow.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NIH reportedly loses two top leaders in two days
item.description: Two key staff members at the National Institutes of Health (NIH) are reportedly leaving the agency amid ongoing efforts by the Trump administration and Department of Government Efficiency head Elon Musk to drastically cut down the size of the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KRON4

item.title: Modi's visit with Trump could boost US-India relations amid tariff woes
item.description: Modi is a nationalist and has talked up his warm relationship with Trump during his first term while cheering his winning back the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Yelp names 'Top 100 Places to Eat' for 2025
item.description: Yelp has once again released its annual rankings of the ‘Top 100 Places to Eat’ in America, perhaps hoping to spare us from eating another mediocre meal over our kitchen sinks.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man whose wife was killed in a hippo attack in Africa sues the US company that booked the trip
item.description: A New Jersey man whose wife was killed in a horrific hippopotamus attack last year during a safari in Africa is suing the U.S. company that arranged the trip, alleging it failed to ensure their safety and did not adequately screen and supervise the tour guides.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs a plan for reciprocal tariffs on US trading partners, ushering in economic uncertainty
item.description: A trade expert claims that Trump's current tariff plan “reflects a fundamental misunderstanding of how the global economy works.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, WGN 9

item.title: Man dies after Las Vegas fire crews find him stuck in palm tree
item.description: A caller reported to Las Vegas Fire and Rescue that they saw an unconscious man about 25 feet in the air and stuck in a palm tree.
item.dc:creator: Julia Romero, NewsNation

item.title: NYC mayor to allow immigration officials to operate at Rikers jail after Trump border czar meeting
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams says he will allow federal immigration officials to operate at the city’s Rikers Island jail.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz And Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: USS Harry S. Truman involved in collision near Egypt
item.description: The USS Harry S. Truman aircraft carrier was involved in a collision Wednesday with a merchant vessel near Port Said, Egypt, in the Mediterranean Sea, the Navy announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate confirms RFK Jr. as Health secretary; McConnell lone GOP dissenter
item.description: Longtime vaccine critic Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is now the nation’s top health official, after the Senate on Thursday voted almost entirely on party lines to confirm him atop a department of nearly 100,000 employees that runs 13 agencies.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KTLA 5

item.title: Denny's to close dozens more restaurants in 2025
item.description: Denny's was already planning to close dozens of locations this year. Now it's planning to close even more.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: A free speech dispute over a New England town's pastry shop painting heads to trial
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A First Amendment dispute over a bright painting that shows sunbeams shining down on a mountain range made of sprinkle-covered chocolate and strawberry doughnuts, a blueberry muffin, a cinnamon roll and other pastries is scheduled for trial Thursday.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
